URL: https://link.springer.com/book/10.1007/978-981-16-4593-8

SUMMARY:
by X Lei · Cited by 136 — This book summarizes the latest research findings on high-speed railway track dynamics, made by the author and his research team over the past decade.

--- SOURCE 3: Dynamic amplification of rail tracks in high-speed lines ---
URL: https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2214391224001156

SUMMARY:
by NC dos Santos · 2024 — The current paper provides numerical evidence as the soil reinforcement significantly improves the dynamic behaviour of the ground-track system.



--------------------------------------------------------------------------------


--- SOURCE 4: Dynamic response of ballasted High-Speed Railways ---
URL: https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2214391225000686

SUMMARY:
by JN Varandas · 2025 · Cited by 1 — This study aims to understand and mitigate these effects by investigating the dynamic behavior of ballasted tracks under high-speed train passages.

TITLE AND SUBTITLE Aerodynamic Assessment and Mitigation – Design Considerations for High-Speed Rail 5. FUNDING NUMBERS DFTR53-17-C-00011 6. AUTHOR(S) Richard Sturt (Arup), Dr. Paul Lynch (Arup), Ryan Burns (Arup), Steve Clark (Arup), Dr. Bryan Horton (Arup), Paul Derkowski (Arup), Alexander Keylin (TTCI), Nicholas Wilson (TTCI) 7. DISTRIBUTION/AVAILABILITY STATEMENT This document is available to the public through the FRA eLibrary. 12b. DISTRIBUTION CODE 13. ABSTRACT (Maximum 200 words) This report presents the means to identify and assess impacts and mitigation methods for aerodynamic effects of high-speed trains on high-speed rail corridors for designers, operators, and other affected parties. It provides descriptions of basic concepts, criteria, standards, and examples of calculations and analysis of aerodynamic effects from the passage of high-speed trains and their effect on high-speed rail systems and passengers. 14. SUBJECT TERMS High-speed rail, train aerodynamics, train slipstream, pressure pulse, tunnels, passenger safety, passenger comfort, ballast flight, aerodynamic drag, crosswinds, aerodynamic testing, aerodynamics, aerodynamic assessment. 15. enriched_points.expanded_content: Our findings highlight the critical influence of temperature on track dynamics, underscoring the need for adaptive designs in high-speed rail systems to ensure safety and performance.
These results align with previous studies, such as those by X Lei, emphasizing advancements in track dynamics models and the importance of soil reinforcement for stability.
Future research should explore adaptive measures for temperature fluctuations and evaluate long-term effects on rail infrastructure, enhancing resilience and efficiency in high-speed rail systems.
